notes: The language formerly known as Perl 6, renamed Raku in Oct 2019 with Larry Wall's blessing for the name. Community pronunciation is RAH-koo — first syllable 'rah' as in cheering 'rah-rah', second 'koo' as in 'cuckoo', stress on RAH. The name honors the Rakudo compiler, a contraction of Japanese Rakuda-dō ('Way of the Camel'), and evokes 楽 raku ('comfort/enjoyment'). English speakers frequently flatten it to RAK-oo (rhyming with 'rack you'), the common Anglicization. Do NOT read it as 'rake-oo'. Note: the cited Hackster.io article covers the rename and etymology but does not itself document the pronunciation.
